mission.title: kong: luarocks not able to install luaossl (or any) packages with latest kong images
mission.description: luarocks not able to install luaossl (or any) packages with latest kong images. Requested by 7+ users.

steps.title: Check current kong deployment
steps.description:
Verify your kong version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kong -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kong
helm list -n kong 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working kong installation.

steps.title: Review kong configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant kong configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n kong -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kong
kubectl get configmap -n kong -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=kong
```
### Is there an existing issue for this?

### Kong version (`$ kong version`)

kong 3.8, kong 3.9, kong 3.10

### Current Behavior

Pul any kong image.

steps.title: Apply the fix for luarocks not able to install luaossl (or any) packages with…
steps.description:
### Summary
### Issue reference

Fix #14598
```yaml
Warning: Failed searching manifest: Failed loading manifest for https://luarocks.org: Error loading file: [string "/var/cache/luarocks/https___luarocks.org/mani..."]:202027: main function has more than 65536 constants
Warning: Failed searching manifest: Failed loading manifest for https://raw.githubusercontent.com/rocks-moonscript-org/moonrocks-mirror/master/: Error loading file: [string "/var/cache/luarocks/https___raw.githubusercon..."]:202027: main function has more than 65536 constants
Warning: Failed searching manifest: Failed loading manifest for https://loadk.com/luarocks/: Error loadin
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kong -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kong
kubectl get events -n kong --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "luarocks not able to install luaossl (or any) packages with…" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary: See the fix PR for the community-verified solution: https://github.com/Kong/kong/pull/14621

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with kong installed or the issue environment reproducible.
